Number agents tick 0 (default 50). max_agents Integer. Hard cap live agents; new offspring rejected exceeded (default 500). max_ticks Integer. Simulation length ticks (default 500).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: energy_init Numeric. Energy agent starts (default 100). energy_max Numeric. Maximum energy agent can hold (default 200). move_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per move action (default 1). idle_cost Numeric. Energy deducted agent move (default 0.5). eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit grass consumed (default 5). min_repro_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required attempt reproduction (default 120). repro_cost Numeric. Energy deducted parent reproduction (default 30). offspring_energy Numeric. Energy given new offspring (default 60). starvation_threshold Numeric. Agent dies energy falls value (default 0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: grass_init_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability cell starts grass (default 0.5). grass_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability empty cell grows grass (default 0.05). grass_max Numeric. Maximum grass units per cell (default 5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: brain type controls agents map sensory inputs action decisions. types heritable evolvable. See references entry primary literature. brain_type Character. One "bnn" (default), "ann", "ctrnn", "grn", "transformer", "synthesis", "random".   hidden_layers Integer vector. Hidden layer widths "ann" "bnn" (default c(8L); gives one hidden layer 8 units). Set c(16L, 8L) two hidden layers. input_radius Integer. Radius Moore neighbourhood used building agent's sensory input vector (default 1L). Radius 1 gives standard 8-cell neighbourhood; radius 2 extends 24-cell neighbourhood. Increasing value expands input vector brain's first-layer width accordingly. n_genes Integer. Number genes "grn" brain type (default 20L). Includes sensory input genes action output genes. transformer_history Integer. Number past sensory inputs transformer attends (default 8L). transformer_heads Integer. Number attention heads (default 2L). synthesis_max_rules Integer. Maximum number -rules per agent (default 10L).
code: * `"bnn"` -- **Bayesian Neural Network** (default). Each synaptic weight   is a probability distribution (mean mu, standard deviation sigma)   rather than a fixed value. The genome encodes the prior (mu, sigma);   within lifetime, experience updates the posterior via approximate   Bayesian inference (sigma shrinks as the agent becomes more certain).   Exploration is automatic: high sigma = uncertain = explore. Diploid   genomes connect naturally: heterozygosity at a locus sets the prior   width for that weight. Implemented via Turing.jl.   References: Neal (1996) *Bayesian Learning for Neural Networks*,   Springer; Blundell et al. (2015) Weight Uncertainty in Neural   Networks, *ICML* pp 1613--1622.  * `"ann"` -- **Multilayer Perceptron**. Standard feedforward network with   fixed weights. Architecture set by `hidden_layers`. Compatible with   the `alifeR` ANN format.   Reference: Rumelhart, Hinton & Williams (1986) Learning   representations by back-propagating errors, *Nature* 323:533--536.  * `"ctrnn"` -- **Continuous-Time Recurrent Neural Network**. Each neuron   i has an internal state y_i governed by the ODE:   tau_i * dy_i/dt = -y_i + sum_j(w_ij * sigma(y_j + theta_j)) + I_i.   Produces temporal dynamics, rhythmic behaviour, and autonomous   initiation. Genome encodes tau (time constants), W (weights), and   theta (biases).   Reference: Beer (1995) On the dynamics of small continuous-time   recurrent neural networks, *Adaptive Behavior* 3(4):469--509.  * `"grn"` -- **Gene Regulatory Network**. The genome IS the brain;   no separate neural network. Each locus represents a gene whose   expression level is regulated by other genes. Some genes receive   sensory input; others produce action output. Behaviour emerges from   ~15-30 gene interaction dynamics simulated each tick. Maximally   biologically minimal. When `ploidy = 2`, each regulatory link has   two alleles and methylation directly suppresses gene expression.   References: Kauffman (1993) *The Origins of Order*, Oxford UP;   Watson & Szathmary (2016) How can evolution learn?, *Trends in   Ecology and Evolution* 31(2):147--157.  * `"transformer"` -- **Attention-Based Transformer**. Attends over a   rolling window of the last `transformer_history` sensory inputs.   Genome encodes query/key/value projection weights and feed-forward   layers. Enables agents to integrate temporal context without explicit   memory parameters.   Reference: Vaswani et al. (2017) Attention is all you need,   *NeurIPS* 30.  * `"synthesis"` -- **Formal / Symbolic Rule Synthesis**. The agent's   brain is an evolved symbolic program: a set of IF-THEN rules of the   form (IF condition_1 AND condition_2 THEN action). Rules are encoded   as a structured list. Evolution proceeds by rule mutation   (add/remove/modify a rule) and rule-set crossover (swap rule subsets   between parents). Behaviour is interpretable and human-readable.   Reference: Angeline, Saunders & Pollack (1994) An evolutionary   algorithm that constructs recurrent neural networks, *IEEE   Transactions on Neural Networks* 5(1):54--65; see also Koza (1992)   *Genetic Programming*, MIT Press.  * `"random"` -- Null brain. Chooses actions uniformly at random.   Used as a baseline to confirm that evolved behaviour outperforms   chance.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Brain energy cost models metabolic cost neural computation, Polyworld simulation (Yaeger 1994). brain_energy_mode Character. One "none", "size", "activity" (default), "prediction_error".   brain_energy_base Numeric. Fixed cost per synaptic weight per tick (default 0.001). brain_energy_activity Numeric. Scaling factor mean absolute activation brain_energy_mode = "activity" (default 0.5).
code: * `"none"` -- no brain energy cost. * `"size"` -- cost proportional to number of synaptic weights. * `"activity"` -- cost = `brain_energy_base * n_weights +   brain_energy_activity * mean(|activations|)`. Larger and more active   brains are more costly. * `"prediction_error"` -- BNN-only; cost proportional to KL divergence   between prior and posterior (measures how much the agent had to   update its beliefs).  Reference: Yaeger (1994) Computational genetics, physiology, metabolism, neural systems, learning, vision, and behavior or PolyWorld: Life in a new context, in *Artificial Life III*, Addison-Wesley, pp 263--298.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: clade supports diploid (default) haploid life cycles. diploid path implements full Mendelian genetics independent assortment recombination. ploidy Integer. 1L = haploid; 2L = diploid (default). diploid case every heritable trait (brain weights, body size, immune strength, cooperation level, etc.) two alleles – one maternal, one paternal. expressed phenotype computed dominance_model birth change within lifetime. n_chromosomes Integer. Number chromosome pairs (default 1L). > 1, chromosomes assort independently meiosis (pair segregates probability 0.5). brain genome split evenly across chromosome pairs. crossover_rate Numeric. Expected number crossover events per chromosome pair per meiosis (Poisson distributed; default 1.0). Set 0 disable recombination. dominance_model Character. maternal paternal alleles combine produce expressed phenotype: * "additive" (default) – phenotype = (maternal + paternal) / 2. * "dominant" – randomly choose one allele locus. * "codominant" – alleles expressed; implemented additive reported separately get_genome_data().
code: Reference: Charlesworth & Charlesworth (2010) *Elements of Evolutionary Genetics*, Roberts & Company, Chapter 5.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian noise added brain weight reproduction (default 0.1). mutation_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, mutation_sd heritable diploid trait evolves (default FALSE). Reference: Sniegowski, Gerrish & Lenski (1997) Evolution high mutation rates experimental populations E. coli, Nature 387:703–705. mutation_sd_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean mutation_sd distribution mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.1). mutation_sd_min Numeric. Minimum allowed mutation_sd (default 0.001). mutation_sd_max Numeric. Maximum allowed mutation_sd (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Within-lifetime learning modifies brain without altering genome. learning_rate_evolution = TRUE, learning rate heritable diploid trait; variation learning_rate across population allows Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896) observed: genomes encode pre-adapted solutions outcompete relying learning. rl_mode Character. Reinforcement learning update rule: "none" (default), "actor_critic", "hebbian".   learning_rate Numeric. Step size within-lifetime weight updates (default 0.01). learning_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, learning_rate heritable diploid trait (default FALSE). Enables study Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896, Hinton & Nowlan 1987).   learning_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean learning_rate distribution learning_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.01). learning_rate_min Numeric. Minimum allowed learning_rate (default 0.0). learning_rate_max Numeric. Maximum allowed learning_rate (default 0.5). plasticity_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit absolute weight change per tick (default 0.05). Implements metabolic cost synaptic plasticity. Reference: Laughlin, de Ruyter van Steveninck & Anderson (1998) metabolic cost neural information, Nature Neuroscience 1(1):36–41. rl_update_freq Integer. Apply RL update every many ticks (default 1L).
code: * `"actor_critic"` -- REINFORCE with baseline (Williams 1992).   Reward = energy delta per tick. Advantage = reward - running mean   (`value_estimate`). Applied to output weights for the chosen action. * `"hebbian"` -- Hebbian potentiation: weights between co-active   neurons are strengthened proportionally to joint activation   (Hebb 1949).  References: Williams (1992) Simple statistical gradient-following algorithms for connectionist reinforcement learning, *Machine Learning* 8:229--256. Hebb (1949) *The Organisation of Behavior*, Wiley. References: Baldwin (1896) A new factor in evolution, *American Naturalist* 30:441--451. Hinton & Nowlan (1987) How learning can guide evolution, *Complex Systems* 1(3):495--502.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: epigenetics = TRUE, agent carries methylome: Boolean vector length genome. element records whether corresponding locus methylated. Within lifetime, learning events can methylate loci (epigenetic_learning_coupling). reproduction, fraction parent's methylation pattern inherited offspring (epigenetic_inheritance). BNN brains, methylation locus shrinks prior sigma_i (canalization – agent becomes less plastic weight). implements transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) described Jablonka & Lamb (2005). epigenetics Logical. Enable epigenetic methylation TEI (default FALSE). Reference: Jablonka & Lamb (2005) Evolution Four Dimensions, MIT Press. epigenetic_learning_coupling Numeric [0, 1]. Probability within-lifetime learning event methylates corresponding locus (default 0.10). epigenetic_inheritance Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction parent's methylation marks inherited offspring (default 0.50). remaining marks reset (demethylated) offspring. epigenetic_effect_size Numeric. Factor methylation reduces prior sigma (BNN) mutational variance locus (brain types) (default 0.20). methylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous methylation probability per locus per tick (default 0.001). demethylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous demethylation probability per methylated locus per tick (default 0.002).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: n_predators_init Integer. Number predators tick 0 (default 0L). Predators evolving brains ("ann" default) co-evolve prey. predator_energy_init Numeric. Starting energy predators (default 150). predator_attack_cost Numeric. Energy deducted predator per attack attempt (default 2). predator_kill_gain Numeric. Energy predator gains per successful kill (default 50). predator_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD predator brain weights (default 0.1). max_predators Integer. Hard cap predator population (default 20L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: life_history Character. "iteroparous" (default) "semelparous". Semelparous agents die immediately reproducing. Reference: Stearns (1992) Evolution Life Histories, Oxford . max_age Integer. Maximum lifespan ticks; agents die age regardless energy (default 200L). Set Inf disable. senescence_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Gompertz mortality rate; per-tick death probability scales exp(senescence_rate * age) (default 0 = senescence). Reference: Gompertz (1825) nature function expressive law human mortality, Philosophical Transactions Royal Society 115:513–583. repro_senescence Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick decline reproduction probability age (default 0 = reproductive senescence). life_history_evolution Logical. TRUE, max_age senescence_rate heritable diploid traits (default FALSE). allee_threshold Integer. Minimum local density reproduction (default 0L = Allee effects ). > 0, agents require least many conspecifics Moore neighbourhood reproduce. Reference: Allee (1931) Animal Aggregations, University Chicago Press.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: body_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable body size trait (default FALSE). Body size scales metabolic costs (larger = expensive), foraging capacity (larger = grass per tick), energy storage (larger = higher cap). Reference size 1.0 (effect). Reference: Kleiber, M. (1947) Body size metabolic rate, Physiological Reviews 27(4):511–541. body_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean body size (default 1.0). body_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD body size (default 0.08). body_size_min Numeric. Minimum body size (default 0.3). body_size_max Numeric. Maximum body size (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: brain_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable brain size trait (default FALSE). Implements parental provisioning hypothesis: brain size metabolically costly (expensive brain hypothesis) yet confers cognitive foraging advantage. bootstrapping problem — large-brained offspring pay metabolic cost birth cognitive advantage can offset — means brain size can evolve parental provisioning (parental_care = TRUE) buffers infancy energy deficit. References: van Schaik et al. (2023) PLoS Biology 21(5):e3002064; Griesser et al. (2023) PNAS 120(31):e2301005120; Song et al. (2025) PNAS 122(8):e2412783122. brain_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean brain size (default 1.0, reference = effect). brain_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD brain size (default 0.05). brain_size_min Numeric. Minimum brain size (default 0.1). brain_size_max Numeric. Maximum brain size (default 3.0). brain_size_cost_scale Numeric. Multiplier metabolic surcharge per unit brain_size - 1.0. Higher values steepen cost curve make bootstrapping problem harder (default 1.0). brain_size_sensing_exponent Numeric. Power applied brain_size scaling grass sensing inputs. brain_size^exponent determines sensing multiplier. Exponent 0 = sensing effect; exponent 1.0 = linear scaling; default 0.3 gives gentle sublinear boost (e.g. brain_size = 2.0 → 1.23× multiplier). effect brain_size_evolution = FALSE.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: metabolic_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable metabolic rate (default FALSE). Scales move_cost idle_cost. metabolic_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean metabolic rate (default 1.0). metabolic_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). metabolic_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.1). metabolic_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 5.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: aging_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable aging rate (default FALSE). Scales Gompertz senescence exponent. aging_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean aging rate (default 1.0). aging_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). aging_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.01). aging_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 10.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: immune_evolution Logical. Enable heritable immune strength trait (default FALSE). immune_strength_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean (default 0.3). immune_strength_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). immune_strength_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.0). immune_strength_max Numeric. Maximum (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: disease Logical. Enable SIR disease dynamics (default FALSE). Reference: Kermack & McKendrick (1927) Contributions mathematical theory epidemics, Proceedings Royal Society 115(772):700–721. disease_seed_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability agent initially infected tick 1 (default 0.01). transmission_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability transmission susceptible neighbour per infected-susceptible adjacent pair per tick (default 0.1). Effective transmission = transmission_prob * (1 - receiver's immune_strength). disease_duration Integer. Ticks agent remains infectious (default 10L). immune_duration Integer. Ticks immunity recovery (default 20L). , agent returns Susceptible. disease_energy_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per tick infected (default 5). disease_death_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability death infected (default 0.02). Scaled (1 - immune_strength) immune_evolution = TRUE.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: kin_selection Logical. Enable kin altruism (default FALSE). Reference: Hamilton (1964) genetical evolution social behaviour & II, Journal Theoretical Biology 7:1–52. kin_altruism_cost Numeric. Energy transferred donor (default 2.0). Hamilton's rule requires benefit * r > cost. kin_altruism_benefit Numeric. Energy received recipient (default 10.0). kin_altruism_r_min Numeric. Minimum pedigree relatedness r required trigger altruism (default 0.25 = half-siblings). kin_altruism_min_donor_energy Numeric. Donor must least energy donate (default 50).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: cooperation_evolution Logical. Enable heritable cooperation level (default FALSE). Uses spatial public goods game (Nowak & May 1992). Reference: Nowak & May (1992) Evolutionary games spatial chaos, Nature 359:826–829. cooperation_multiplier Numeric. Payoff multiplier M public goods game (default 2.0). cooperator pays cost C; group receives M * sum(contributions) / n_local. cooperation selectively favoured, M must exceed group size. cooperation_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean cooperation level (default 0.5). cooperation_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). cooperation_cost Numeric. Per-tick energy cost paid cooperator (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: dispersal_evolution Logical. Enable heritable natal dispersal (default FALSE). agent carries dispersal_tendency trait (per-tick probability moving away birthplace). Reduces inbreeding kin competition. Reference: Ronce, O. (2007) feel like rolling stone? Annual Review Ecology, Evolution, Systematics 38:231–253. dispersal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per dispersal step (default 2.0). Agents energy <= 2 x dispersal_cost disperse. dispersal_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean dispersal tendency (default 0.1). dispersal_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD dispersal tendency (default 0.02). dispersal_min Numeric. Minimum dispersal tendency (default 0.0). dispersal_max Numeric. Maximum dispersal tendency (default 0.5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: parental_care Logical. Enable altricial parental care (default FALSE). Offspring remain parent graduation. care_duration Integer. Ticks offspring remain parental care (default 5L). care_cost_per_offspring Numeric. Energy cost per carried offspring per tick (default 2.0). max_clutch_size Integer. Maximum offspring per reproductive event (default 1L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: signal_dims Integer. Number signal dimensions (default 0L = ). > 0, agent evolves heritable signal vector preference vector; mates chosen proximity signal preference. signal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit signal magnitude per tick (default 0.1). Models honest signalling costs (Zahavi 1975). Reference: Zahavi (1975) Mate selection – selection handicap, Journal Theoretical Biology 53(1):205–214.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: mimicry Logical. Enable heritable toxicity trait predator signal learning (default FALSE). Implements Mullerian Batesian mimicry via Rescorla-Wagner learning predators. Reference: Rescorla & Wagner (1972) theory Pavlovian conditioning, Classical Conditioning II, Appleton-Century-Crofts, pp 64–99.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: niche_construction Logical. Enable shelter building (default FALSE). Shelters reduce predation slow grass regrowth. Reference: Odling-Smee, Laland & Feldman (2003) Niche Construction: Neglected Process Evolution, Princeton . shelter_build_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability building shelter unit per tick eligible (default 0.1). shelter_max_depth Integer. Maximum shelter depth per cell (default 5L). shelter_min_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required build (default 80). shelter_decay_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability shelter unit decays (default 0.05).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: scavenging Logical. Enable carrion dynamics (default FALSE). Dead agents deposit body mass carrion; scavengers consume . carrion_fraction Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction energy_init deposited carrion upon agent death (default 0.5). Uses energy_init agent$energy death, body mass change starvation. carrion_decay_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick fraction carrion decomposes (default 0.1). carrion_eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit carrion consumed (default 3).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: group_defense Logical. Enable dilution-effect predator avoidance (default FALSE). Per-agent predation probability decreases local group size. Reference: Foster & Treherne (1981) Evidence dilution effect selfish herd fish predation marine insect, Nature 293:466–467.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: habitat_preference_evolution Logical. Enable heritable habitat preference (default FALSE). Preference encodes optimal local grass density; agents attracted cells matching preference. Reference: Fretwell & Lucas (1970) territorial behavior factors influencing habitat distribution birds, Acta Biotheoretica 19(1):16–36.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: seasonal_amplitude Numeric. Amplitude sinusoidal grass_rate modulation (default 0 = ). grass_rate_t = grass_rate * (1 + seasonal_amplitude * sin(2 * pi * t / seasonal_period)). seasonal_period Integer. Period seasonal cycle ticks (default 100L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: speciation Logical. Enable reproductive isolation based genome distance (default FALSE). Agents mate conspecifics (genome distance < isolation_threshold). Reference: Gavrilets (2004) Fitness Landscapes Origin Species, Princeton . isolation_threshold Numeric. Maximum genome distance successful mating (default 0.45). Species inferred hierarchical clustering genome distances logging tick.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: social_learning Logical. Enable copying output-layer weights successful neighbours (default FALSE). Implements prestige bias. Reference: Laland (2004) Social learning strategies, Learning Behavior 32(1):4–14. social_learning_freq Integer. Apply social learning every many ticks (default 10L). social_learning_rate Numeric. Fraction neighbour weights copied (default 0.1).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: world_evolution = TRUE, environment parameters listed world_params_to_evolve treated evolvable quantities change via random drift tick, allowing environment co-evolve agents. extends MAP-Elites search joint agent-environment space. world_evolution Logical. Enable world parameter evolution (default FALSE). world_mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian perturbations world parameters per tick (default 0.02). world_params_to_evolve Character vector. Names parameters default_specs() treat evolvable. Example: c("grass_rate", "disease_death_prob"). Defaults character(0) (none).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Adds shrub canopy resource layers top ground grass. Canopy access requires wing_size >= canopy_threshold (heritable). Designed break standard brain-size x longevity correlation. Reference: Liedtke & Fromhage (2019); Isbell (2006). complex_landscape Logical. Enable multi-layer resources (default FALSE). shrub_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial fraction cells shrub resource (default 0.3). shrub_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth shrubs (default 0.03). shrub_energy Numeric. Maximum energy shrub cell provides (default 20.0). canopy_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial canopy coverage (default 0.15; trees sparse). canopy_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth canopy (default 0.005; trees grow slowly). canopy_energy Numeric. Maximum energy canopy cell provides (default 50.0; high energy density aerial specialists). canopy_threshold Numeric. Minimum wing_size needed canopy access (default 0.6). wing_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean wing_size (default 0.0; ground-bound founders). wing_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD wing_size (default 0.05). wing_size_min Numeric. Minimum wing size (default 0.0). wing_size_max Numeric. Maximum wing size (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Implements assortative mating invasion range front, causing dispersal-enhancing alleles accumulate frontier without requiring fitness advantage sedentary conspecifics. Reference: Shine et al. (2011) evolutionary process assembles phenotypes space rather time. PNAS 108:5708–5711. Requires dispersal_evolution = TRUE effect. spatial_sorting Logical. Enable range-front mate preference high-dispersal partners (default FALSE). sorting_front_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction maximum range radius defines "front" (default 0.75; outermost 25%). sorting_mating_boost Numeric. Multiplier applied dispersal_tendency scoring mates front (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: IFfolk = offspring + sum(r x relative's offspring). Agents transfer energy energy-depleted kin. Optional parliament suppression penalises defectors cooperative populations. Reference: Fromhage & Jennions (2019) strategic reference gene. Proc R Soc B 286:20190459. doi:10.1098/rspb.2019.0459 iffolk_selection Logical. Enable IFfolk energy transfers (default FALSE). iffolk_r_min Numeric. Minimum relatedness coefficient relative count (default 0.125 = cousin). iffolk_radius Integer. Spatial search radius relatives grid cells (default 5L). iffolk_transfer Numeric. Maximum energy transferred per altruistic act (default 3.0). iffolk_min_energy Numeric. Minimum donor energy required transfer (default 60.0). parliament_suppression Logical. Enable parliament--genes penalty defectors (negative helper_tendency) surrounded cooperators (default FALSE). parliament_cost Numeric. Energy cost per tick defectors cooperative neighbourhoods (default 0.5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: log_freq Integer. Log population-level statistics every many ticks (default 1L; set higher reduce memory use long runs). log_genomes Logical. Log flattened genome vectors get_genome_data()$genomes log tick (default FALSE; memory intensive large populations). random_seed Integer NA_integer_. Seed Julia's RNG. NA_integer_ uses random seed (default).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
code: specs <- default_specs() specs$brain_type   <- "bnn" specs$ploidy       <- 2L specs$n_agents_init <- 100L # run_alife(specs)   # requires Julia

title: Visualise the multi-trait diversity landscape — diversity_landscape

what: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: estimate_heritability() returns coarse estimate narrow-sense heritability (\(h^2\)) quantitative trait logged per tick run_alife(). estimator lag-1 temporal autocorrelation population mean, $$\hat{h}^2 \approx \mathrm{cor}(\bar{z}_t,\, \bar{z}_{t+1}),$$ used proxy parent-offspring regression (Falconer & Mackay 1996, ch. 10). proxy reasonable () trait directional stabilising selection, (ii) generation overlap moderate, (iii) logging interval short relative generation time. exact quantitative-genetic estimate reported one. exact estimate requires parent-offspring pairs, clade currently log.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
code: estimate_heritability(run_data, trait = "body_size")

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: run_data list returned get_run_data() (must contain $ticks column paste0("mean_", trait)). trait Character. trait name (without mean_ prefix). Defaults "body_size". column form mean_<trait> run_data$ticks supported (e.g. "immune_strength", "metabolic_rate", "learning_rate").

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: list components: $h2 Numeric. Lag-1 autocorrelation mean_<trait>. Returns NA_real_ series fewer three usable values zero variance. $method Character constant "lag1_autocorrelation". $trait trait name supplied caller. $n Integer. Number paired observations used. $note Character. reminder proxy exact estimate requires parent-offspring logging.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: Falconer, D.S. & Mackay, T.F.C. (1996) Introduction Quantitative Genetics, 4th ed. Longman, Harlow.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ env  <- run_alife(default_specs()) rd   <- get_run_data(env) estimate_heritability(rd, trait = "body_size") estimate_heritability(rd, trait = "immune_strength") } # }

code: full_specs()

what: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: Creates 0/1 wall matrix use run_alife(). Five landscape types available, ranging completely open structured corridor networks. Pass result prepare_map() validate dimensions use.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
code: generate_map(   type = "random_cluster",   grid_rows = 50L,   grid_cols = 50L,   p_wall = 0.3,   scale = 4,   corridor_width = 2L,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: type Character; one "open", "patchy", "random_cluster", "gaussian_field", "corridors". Default "random_cluster". grid_rows Integer; number grid rows. Default 50. grid_cols Integer; number grid columns. Default 50. p_wall Numeric (0, 1); target proportion wall cells. Default 0.3. Ignored "open". scale Positive numeric; spatial scale wall clustering (sigma Gaussian, autocorrelation range nlm_gaussianfield). Default 4. corridor_width Integer; open-corridor width cells ("corridors" ). Default 2. seed Integer NULL; random seed. Default NULL.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: integer matrix (0 = open, 1 = wall) dimensions grid_rows x grid_cols. Assign specs$wall_map calling run_alife(), pass prepare_map() first.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: Types "random_cluster" "gaussian_field" use NLMR package installed; otherwise pure-R Gaussian-smoothed fallback used. NLMR removed CRAN; install ropensci/NLMR (e.g. remotes::install_github("ropensci/NLMR")) want higher-quality neutral-landscape generators.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
code: map <- generate_map("random_cluster", grid_rows = 20L, grid_cols = 20L,                     p_wall = 0.25, seed = 1L) mean(map == 0L)  # fraction open #> [1] 0.75

title: Compute normalised Euclidean genome distance between two agents — genome_distance

text: list : $h2 Estimated heritability (slope parent-offspring regression). NA fewer 5 matched pairs found. $n_pairs Number matched parent-offspring pairs. $method "parent_offspring_regression". $trait trait used.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from parent-offspring data — heritability_estimate

title: Inspect the brain structure of a single agent — inspect_brain
text: named list elements: $brain_type Character. Value env$specs$brain_type. $n_layers Integer. Number layers brain$layers. $layer_sizes List integer vectors c(nrow, ncol) layer weight matrix. $n_weights Integer. Total number weight values across layers. $weight_mean Numeric. Mean weights. $weight_sd Numeric. Standard deviation weights. $weight_min Numeric. Minimum weight value. $weight_max Numeric. Maximum weight value. $sigma_mean, $sigma_sd, $sigma_min, $sigma_max Numeric. Statistics $sigma matrices (BNN brains ). $note Character. Present brain$layers unavailable; explains summary statistics absent.

title: Inspect the brain structure of a single agent — inspect_brain

what: Objective function for the complex landscape scenario — objective_complex_landscape
title: Objective function for the complex landscape scenario — objective_complex_landscape
text: Measures well complex-landscape module working combining: (1) wing size evolution (late mean - early mean), (2) niche diversity entropy across ground/shrub/canopy layers, (3) log-survival bonus. Returns -Inf extinct populations runs fewer 10 active ticks, making safe pass directly search_cmaes().

what: Plot genetic diversity over the run — plot_diversity
title: Plot genetic diversity over the run — plot_diversity
text: Draws trajectory mean pairwise genome distance (genetic diversity) , body-size evolution active, coefficient variation body size proxy phenotypic diversity. series scaled 0-1 range visual comparison. common pattern high diversity founders, selective sweep reduces diversity best foraging strategy spreads, partial recovery population niches. Permanent low diversity indicates genetic drift clonal selection; permanently high diversity indicates balancing selection frequency-dependent dynamics.

what: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run — plot_module_metrics
title: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run — plot_module_metrics
text: Detects optional simulation modules active run inspecting logged tick columns run_data$ticks, assembles six panels patchwork grid. panels modules produced non-zero data included: Predators n_predators – line plot predator population time. Species n_species – step plot species count; shown speciation active (max > 1). Traits Overlay mean_toxicity, mean_plasticity, mean_helper_tendency three coloured lines. Signals mean_signal_magnitude – line plot; shown signal evolution active (max > 0). Parental care n_juveniles – line plot juveniles care; shown max > 0. Mimicry n_toxic_attacks n_avoided_attacks – two lines showing attacks versus avoidance events; shown mimicry attacks occurred (max n_toxic_attacks > 0). fewer two panels active, single informative placeholder plot returned instead.

what: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap — plot_weight_heatmap
title: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap — plot_weight_heatmap
text: Renders weight matrix ANN-format brain (list layers element, layer $W $b) diverging blue-white-red tile heatmap. Blue = strong inhibitory connections, red = strong excitatory connections, white = near-zero weights. One panel per layer. Agents strong selection often show structured weight matrices (certain input--hidden connections consistently strong) compared near-random patterns newly initialised founders.

text: fn function signature function(env) -> env. Character; one "pre_tick", "post_agents", "post_tick", "post_reproduce". Default "post_tick". name Character label module (optional; used list_modules() output).

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
text: Invisibly NULL. Side-effects: adds module registry.

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
text: Custom modules can modify: env$agents[[]]$energy — agent energy env$agents[[]]$alive — set FALSE kill agent env$grass — resource matrix (numeric matrix, rows x cols)

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
code: # Kill any agent older than 100 ticks register_module(   fn   = function(env) {     for (i in seq_along(env$agents)) {       if (isTRUE(env$agents[[i]]$age > 100)) env$agents[[i]]$alive <- FALSE     }     env   },   when = "post_tick",   name = "max_age_100" ) clear_modules()

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: run_alife() primary entry point clade. sends specs Julia , runs specs$max_ticks ticks entirely Julia, returns environment list containing final agent population logged statistics. Julia session started automatically first call.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
code: run_alife(specs = default_specs(), verbose = TRUE)

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: specs named list simulation parameters, typically default_specs() modifications. parameters documented default_specs(). verbose Logical. Print progress messages (default TRUE). Pass FALSE batch runs testing.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: env list components: $agents list agent lists, one per surviving agent. $t Final tick number (equals specs$max_ticks). $specs specs list used run (may differ input world_evolution = TRUE). $progress data frame per-tick logged statistics (get_run_data(env)$ticks). $deaths data frame per-death records (get_run_data(env)$deaths). $genome_log list per-tick genome matrices (non-NULL specs$log_genomes = TRUE).

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: R-Julia boundary crossed per call regardless simulation length agent count. contrasts Rcpp-based simulators (including alifeR) data marshalled across R-C++ boundary every tick.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ specs <- default_specs() env   <- run_alife(specs) data  <- get_run_data(env) plot_run(data)  # Diploid run specs$ploidy <- 2L env2 <- run_alife(specs)  # BNN with epigenetics specs$brain_type  <- "bnn" specs$epigenetics <- TRUE env3 <- run_alife(specs) } # }

what: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: Implements agent-based model Smaldino & McElreath (2016). "Labs" (agent groups) heritable research_power (W; probability detecting real effect hypothesis test true) research_effort (e; methodological rigour, lowers per-test false-positive rate). tick, lab tests n_studies_per_tick hypotheses, priori true probability base_rate_true (b). Pub counts

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
code: run_bad_science(   n_labs = 200L,   n_ticks = 500L,   n_studies_per_tick = 5L,   replication_rate = 0,   replication_penalty = 5,   alpha_base = 0.5,   base_rate_true = 0.3,   research_power_init_mean = 0.3,   research_effort_init_mean = 0.8,   mutation_sd = 0.05,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: n_labs Integer; number labs. Default 200. n_ticks Integer; number evolutionary ticks run. Default 500. n_studies_per_tick Integer; studies produced per lab per tick. Default 5. replication_rate Numeric [0, 1]; probability per tick lab attempts replicate one random neighbour's published findings. failed replication (original false positive) debits original lab's publication count replication_penalty. Default 0. replication_penalty Numeric \(\geq 0\); publication credits subtracted original lab replication attempt fails. Set 0 recover "replication without cost" behaviour. Default 5. alpha_base Numeric (0, 1]; per-test false-positive rate zero effort. FPR effort e alpha_base * (1 - e). Default 0.5, matching Smaldino & McElreath's \(\alpha_0\). base_rate_true Numeric (0, 1); prior probability tested hypothesis genuinely true. Default 0.3. Higher values mean larger share publications true positives, sharpens replication's ability discriminate low-effort labs; low values (< 0.15) swamp replication's signal almost every publication false positive regardless effort. research_power_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research power W. Default 0.3. research_effort_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research effort e. Default 0.8. mutation_sd Numeric; standard deviation Gaussian mutation applied traits reproduction. Default 0.05. seed Integer NULL; random seed reproducibility. Default NULL.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: data frame one row per tick columns: t Tick number. mean_power Mean research_power across labs. mean_effort Mean research_effort across labs. mean_fpr Mean false-positive rate alpha across labs. total_publications Total publications produced tick. failed_replications Number failed replication attempts.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: true positives  ~ Binomial(n_studies * b,       W) false positives ~ Binomial(n_studies * (1 - b), alpha(e)) alpha(e) = alpha_base * (1 - e). per-test FPR depends effort , W, matching Smaldino & McElreath's formulation. Labs publications reproduce higher rates. replication_rate > 0, lab probability per tick replicating random peer's random finding; failed replication (original false positive, happens probability alpha(1-b) / (Wb + alpha(1-b))) debits original lab's publication count replication_penalty. large enough penalty, replication culture selects low-effort labs slows reverses evolution high FPR.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: Smaldino, P.E. & McElreath, R. (2016) natural selection bad science. Royal Society Open Science 3:160384. doi:10.1098/rsos.160384

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
code: result <- run_bad_science(n_ticks = 200L, seed = 1L) plot(result$t, result$mean_fpr, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Mean false-positive rate",      main = "Evolution of bad science")

what: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: Optimises scalar objective function simulation parameter space using pure-R implementation Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (CMA-ES; Hansen & Ostermeier 2001). Unlike MAP-Elites, CMA-ES finds single optimal parameter set adapting search distribution local curvature objective landscape.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
code: search_cmaes(   specs_base,   objective = "genetic_diversity",   params = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   n_iterations = 200L,   popsize = NULL,   sigma0 = 0.3,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). objective Character function (search_map_elites()). params Character vector positive numeric parameter names optimise. Defaults c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"). n_iterations Integer. Maximum CMA-ES generations (default 200L). popsize Integer NULL. CMA-ES population size lambda. NULL (default), uses standard formula max(8, 4 + floor(3 * log(n_params))). sigma0 Numeric. Initial step size log scale (default 0.3). n_cores Integer. Parallel cores candidate evaluation (default 1L). Uses parallel::mclapply() Unix/macOS > 1. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: list : $specs Best specs encountered across generations. $score Best objective value encountered. $history Data frame one row per generation columns generation, evals, best_score, mean_score, sigma.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: Parameters optimised log scale positive constraints always satisfied. generation evaluates lambda candidate parameter sets, selects best mu = lambda/2, updates mean, step size, covariance matrix. external packages required.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: Hansen, N. & Ostermeier, . (2001) Completely derandomized self-adaptation evolution strategies. Evolutionary Computation 9(2):159-195. Hansen, N. (2006) CMA evolution strategy: comparing review. : Towards New Evolutionary Computation, Springer, pp 75-102.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_cmaes(   default_specs(),   objective    = "genetic_diversity",   params       = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   n_iterations = 50L ) result$specs$grass_rate result$history   # one row per generation } # }

what: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: Optimises scalar objective respect named numeric simulation parameters using forward-difference gradient ascent. gradient coordinate estimated re-running run_alife() one parameter perturbed epsilon log scale, comparing resulting objective baseline run. Updates applied log scale positive parameters remain positive.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
code: search_gradient(   specs_base,   params = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   objective = "genetic_diversity",   n_steps = 20L,   epsilon = 0.05,   learning_rate = 0.1,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). params Character vector numeric parameter names optimise. Defaults c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"). objective Character function. character, name column get_run_data()$ticks maximise. function, must accept env list return numeric scalar. n_steps Integer. Number gradient ascent steps (default 20L). epsilon Numeric. Finite-difference step log scale (default 0.05). learning_rate Numeric. Log-scale step size parameter updates (default 0.1). n_cores Integer. Reserved future parallel finite-difference evaluation; currently unused (default 1L). verbose Logical. Print progress (default TRUE).

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: list components: $specs Best specs encountered across gradient steps. $score Best objective value encountered. $history Data frame one row per step columns step, score, one column per optimised parameter.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: implementation intentionally backend-agnostic: treats run_alife() black box requires (n_steps + 1) * n_params simulation calls per run. true gradient--simulation approach using Zygote.jl automatic differentiation Julia backend, see deferred Phase 4b plan.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: Spall, J.C. (1998) overview simultaneous perturbation method efficient optimization. Johns Hopkins APL Technical Digest 19(4):482-492. Innes, M. (2018) unroll adjoint: differentiating SSA-form programs. arXiv:1810.07951. (Zygote.jl – Phase 4b deferred.)

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_gradient(   default_specs(),   params        = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   objective     = "genetic_diversity",   n_steps       = 20L,   epsilon       = 0.05,   learning_rate = 0.1 ) result$specs$grass_rate } # }

what: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: Finds diverse archive high-performing parameter combinations using MAP-Elites algorithm (Mouret & Clune 2015). Rather finding one optimal parameter set, MAP-Elites maintains grid niches behaviour space; cell holds best-performing specs behavioural region.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
code: search_map_elites(   specs_base,   archive_dims,   n_iterations = 1000L,   objective = "genetic_diversity",   mutation_params = NULL,   mutation_sd = 0.1,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). Used starting point mutations. archive_dims named list whose names column names get_run_data()$ticks whose values numeric vectors specifying bin breakpoints dimension. Example: list(genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, = 0.1), n_species = 1:10). n_iterations Integer. Number MAP-Elites iterations (default 1000L). objective Character function. character, name column get_run_data()$ticks maximise (e.g. "genetic_diversity"). function, must accept env list return numeric scalar. mutation_params Character vector parameter names (default_specs()) mutate. Defaults numeric parameters. mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian perturbations log-transformed parameter values (default 0.1). n_cores Integer. Parallel cores batch evaluation (default 1L). verbose Logical. Print progress (default TRUE).

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: list components: $archive list lists, one per archive cell, containing: $specs, $score, $behavioural_descriptor (named numeric vector). $map ggplot2 heatmap archive scores (2D archive dims ). NULL higher-dimensional archives. $history Data frame one row per iteration: iteration, score, filled_cells.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: Initialise: fill archive cell sampling random specs near specs_base. n_iterations iterations: . Select random filled cell archive. b. Mutate specs (Gaussian perturbation numeric parameters). c. Run run_alife(new_specs). d. Compute behavioural descriptors get_run_data(env). e. new specs score better current occupant corresponding archive cell, replace . Return full archive.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: Mouret, J.-B. & Clune, J. (2015) Illuminating search spaces mapping elites. arXiv:1504.04909. Chatzilygeroudis, K., Cully, ., Vassiliades, V. & Mouret, J.-B. (2021) Quality-Diversity Optimization: novel branch stochastic optimization. arXiv:2012.04322.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_map_elites(   specs_base   = default_specs(),   archive_dims = list(     genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),     n_species         = 1:10   ),   n_iterations = 500L,   objective    = "genetic_diversity" ) result$map   # ggplot2 heatmap } # }

Optional second parameter (creates 2D grid supplied). values_y Numeric vector values param_y. Ignored param_y = NULL. n_reps Integer. Replicates per grid cell (different random seeds; default 3L). survival_threshold Numeric. Fraction initial agents must survive run count viable (default 0.1). objective Character function NULL. supplied, mean objective score across surviving replicates added column mean_objective (default NULL). verbose Logical (default TRUE).

Tests vignettes skipped (require Julia, GH runners); check still catches package-level issues (namespace, docs, DESCRIPTION). .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml builds deploys pkgdown site push main/master release publication.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: R/visualization.R: extracted .plot_empty(message) helper, replaced nine duplicated “theme_void annotation” fallback blocks. File ~40 lines shorter; behavior byte-identical. inst/julia/src/reproduce.jl: dropped stale “Phase 2 stub” remark referred graduate_offspring!() wired commit 7ad2b1d.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: vignettes/showcase.Rmd: Baldwin section gains honest “σ rises defaults; CMA-ES finds narrowing regime” caveat; Mimicry Niche sections document new 0.3.0 flags. vignettes/introduction.Rmd, vignettes/scenarios.Rmd: gallery tables surface batesian_mimicry shelter_occupancy_bonus. vignettes/s-mimicry.Rmd, vignettes/s-niche.Rmd: Key-parameters tables gain new flags; --found sections refreshed describe current (post-0.3.0) kernel rather pre-fix state. vignettes/s-parental-care.Rmd, s-parental-investment.Rmd: re-measured displayed specs fixed graduation pathway; --found quotes updated “n_juveniles = 0 throughout” actual current trajectories. vignettes/baldwin-effect.Rmd: Addendum section documents CMA-ES-discovered regime canalization emerges (grass_rate 0.027, learning_rate_init_mean 0.007). vignettes/figures/showcase_bnn_canalization_demo.png: previously orphaned (generator produced ); now emitted gen_fixed_patch_fig.R original filename canalization alias, keeping Baldwin articles sync live kernel biology.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: Batesian mimicry (specs$batesian_mimicry = TRUE). palatable mimic (toxicity = 0) whose signal matches toxic model species now benefits predator’s learned aversion — predator avoids mimic alongside true toxic prey. Repeated attacks palatable mimics decay predator’s aversion memory (Rescorla-Wagner toward 0), reproducing Bates–Wallace predator discrimination-learning cycle. Default FALSE — existing runs byte-identical. Reference: Bates (1862) Trans. Linn. Soc. 23:495–566; Ruxton, Sherratt & Speed (2004) Avoiding Attack. Heritable niche-construction benefit (specs$shelter_occupancy_bonus > 0). Agents occupying sheltered cell receive bonus × depth energy per tick, Odling-Smee, Laland & Feldman (2003) heritable-environment effect. Existing niche_construction semantics (grass suppression + persistence + decay) unchanged; new parameter additive defaults 0 (behavior change).

title: clade 0.3.0
text: R/run.R .validate_specs() now warns spatial_sorting = TRUE combined toroidal = TRUE. Shine et al. (2011) invasion-front dynamics require bounded grid; torus population centroid wraps “front” concept geometrically ill-posed. toxicity_cost_per_tick default raised 0.5 → 2.0. prior default equalled idle_cost, making toxicity effectively free (Zahavi 1975 handicap principle violated). 2.0 makes maximally-toxic agent pay ~4× idle baseline per tick. inst/julia/src/modules/disease.jl docstring now documents transmission density-dependent (per-neighbour contact, β · S · / N frequency-dependent SIR) disease_duration deterministic recovery period (delta-distribution, exponential). Includes mean-field R0 ≈ transmission_prob × 8 × disease_duration approximation.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: New dev/audit/calibration/ harness drives search_cmaes() scenario’s parameter subspace, per-scenario fitness function encoding biological claim. Parallel launcher (run_all.sh) runs 31 scenarios concurrently. Full Phase 7 results dev/audit/calibration/RESULTS.md. Headline discoveries: regime Baldwin effect emerge (grass_rate ≈ 0.027, learning_rate_init_mean ≈ 0.007) otherwise non-canalising foraging world; regime speciation fires (mutation_sd ≈ 1.31); cephalopod paradox (Liedtke & Fromhage 2019) reproduced short max_age high learning rate.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: systematic end--end audit 35 scenario vignettes vignettes/s-*.Rmd performed. vignette’s displayed code run live Julia kernel, oracle metric trajectory inspected, outcome classified. Full audit machinery ships dev/audit/ can rerun. Final state: 30 scenarios OK, 2 aggregate galleries (s-cross-module, s-module-comparison) design without direction oracle, 2 search-scenarios (s-bad-science, s-map-elites) design without trajectory output, 1 documented limitation (s-stress-hypermutation, flat mutation rate displayed defaults).

title: clade 0.3.0
text: BNN REINFORCE score function fixed. previous update rule mu[] += lr * advantage * sigma[], score function Gaussian policy. new rule mu[] += lr * advantage * (w_sample[] - mu[]) / sigma[]^2 matches exact score Williams (1992) Blundell et al. (2015) §3.2. new last_sample::Vector{Float32} field BNNBrain caches Thompson-sampled weights forward() use bnn_update!. likely main reason earlier runs showed Baldwin-effect sigma narrowing even favourable regimes. Parliament--genes penalty logic fixed modules/kin.jl. counter previously included cooperators across neighbours; now counts cooperative kin separately, penalty fires cooperative relatives outnumber non-cooperative relatives — consistent Haig (2000) intragenomic conflict suppression. Predator dedicated sensory architecture. seed_predators! now builds predator-specific brain architecture [15, hidden..., 5] brain’s n_inputs matches 15-element predator sense vector. Previously predators used prey’s architecture (dynamic size depending input_radius active sensory modules), producing silent dimension mismatch whenever defaults changed. Mutation-rate evolution uses per-agent trait. mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE, reproduce.jl now reads parent.mutation_sd base mutation rate meiosis. Previously global specs["mutation_sd"] used unconditionally, evolved trait never propagated. stress-hypermutation multiplier continues apply top. Parental-care graduation pathway wired . reproduce.jl:126-131 Phase 2 stub pushed offspring straight env.agents even parental_care = TRUE. Offspring now enter parent’s carried_offspring brood (care_load += 1), age via age_juveniles!, fed via feed_offspring!, graduate main agent pool via graduate_offspring! reach juvenile_independence_age juvenile_independence_energy. fix, n_juveniles always 0 s-parental-care s-parental-investment; , typical runs log hundreds carried juveniles. randperm now imported Clade.jl. Latent bug surfaced parental-care fix enabled graduate_offspring! code path: randperm referenced modules/parental_care.jl:153 imported using Random line. Include-order fix (inst/julia/src/brains/ann.jl → bnn.jl). _quantize_brain_weights!(::BNNBrain, ...) method defined ann.jl bnn.jl loaded; Julia 1.12 aborts module load UndefVarError: BNNBrain. method now lives bnn.jl alongside type, matching method’s dispatch target. Manifest regenerated Julia ≥ 1.11 compatibility (previously pinned Statistics 1.10 doesn’t ship 1.12). Project.toml unchanged.

DESCRIPTION: tidyr added Suggests vignettes/s-kitchen-sink.Rmd vignettes/s-seasonal.Rmd call tidyr::pivot_longer().

title: clade 0.3.0
text: Rmd parser (parse_rmd.R) extracts displayed chunks, figure refs, “found” prose every scenario. Scenario oracle (scenario_oracle.R) maps vignettes expected module flags direction-signal oracle. entry carries comment documenting oracle phrased , grounded vignette’s reported findings (several vignettes document honest negative results oracle matches ). Serial driver (run_audit.R) runs scenarios one warm Julia session — parallel forks deadlock JuliaConnectoR’s socket; sequential reliable completes ~5–7 min. Text-drift scanner (text_drift_scan.R) catches displayed-vs-prose numeric drift without running simulation. Consolidation survey (consolidation_report.md) enumerates ~550 lines refactor opportunities across R/ inst/julia/src/modules/; scheduled 0.3.0. tests/testthat/test-scenario-signals.R asserts direction-signals every scenario oracle, robust seed noise.

what: Known limitations surfaced by the audit
title: clade 0.3.0
text: documented vignette’s “found” section intentionally preserved rather papered : BNN prior sigma rises rather narrows competitive foraging world (s-baldwin). Baldwin effect formalised Hinton & Nowlan (1987) requires stable global fitness peak; clade’s default world provide one. vignette’s 45-run 3×3 factorial plus run-length experiments (500/1000/2000/5000 ticks) establish rigorously. stress_hypermutation scales specs["mutation_sd"] transiently reproduction rather mutating per-agent ag.mutation_sd field, logged mean_mutation_rate stays flat even mechanism fires. observable signal transient rise genetic_diversity resource crashes.

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)
text: plasticity_init_mean default changed 0.0 0.3. previous default sat trait floor; mutation pressure push upward, mean_plasticity always 0 practice.  signal_evolution_drift default changed FALSE TRUE. Without drift, signals remained exactly 0 forever, making mate-choice experiments uninformative.

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)
text: Lamarckian evolution (lamarckian = TRUE): rl_mode "none", within-lifetime RL-updated brain weights written back parent’s genome meiosis offspring inherit learned solution directly. Implemented inst/julia/src/modules/lamarckian.jl. Distinct epigenetics module (inherits methylation marks, weight values) Baldwin Effect (leaves genome unchanged). References: Baldwin (1896); Weismann (1892); Jablonka & Lamb (2005). Discrete / quantized ANN weights (ann_weight_values): set numeric vector (e.g. c(-1, 0, 1) ternary weights), every synaptic weight bias snapped nearest allowed value genome expression. Applies "ann" "bnn" brain types. Biologically motivated evidence biological synapses operate discrete strength states (Bhumbra & Bhatt 2020). Enables symbolic formula distillation evolved ANNs (original MATLAB alife2025usra codebase). ANN weight regularisation (ann_regularization): per-tick energy penalty brain weight complexity. Two modes: "weight_magnitude" (L1 penalty, drives weights toward zero) "weight_count" (L0-like penalty, fixed cost per active synapse). Scaled ann_regularization_lambda (default 0.001). mean weight magnitude now logged every run mean_ann_weight_magnitude. References: Laughlin et al. (1998); Attwell & Laughlin (2001). Native Julia test directory (inst/julia/test/): unit tests quantization, regularisation, Lamarckian logic can run directly Julia without R side. Run julia --project=inst/julia inst/julia/test/runtests.jl. Module triage script (inst/scripts/triage_modules.R): runs module isolation 300 ticks reports whether key biological signal detected ([OK]), absent ([FLAT]), crashing ([ERR]). Run Rscript inst/scripts/triage_modules.R. expect_evolution() test helper (tests/testthat/helper.R): asserts logged trait moves directionally simulation run. Also consolidates duplicated skip_no_julia() definition helper.R individual test files longer need re-define . Non-toroidal grid (toroidal = FALSE): Julia modules now use wrap_or_clamp() helper either wraps (toroidal) clamps grid boundary (linear). Required spatial sorting invasion-front experiments defined front. Carrion pathogen reservoir (carrion_transmission_prob): scavenging = TRUE disease = TRUE, agents die infected deposit flagged carcass; agent scavenges becomes infected probability carrion_transmission_prob (default 0.0 — backwards compatibility). batch_seeds(): convenience wrapper around batch_alife() takes single specs object vector seeds returns named list results. quick_specs() / full_specs(): preset specs fast exploratory runs (50 agents, 200 ticks, 20×20 grid) publication-quality runs (200 agents, 1000 ticks, 30×30 grid).

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)
text: Six previously-NA log columns now populated: mean_relatedness — mean pairwise relatedness kin_selection = TRUE. n_scavenge_events — number carrion-eating events per tick. n_gd_events — number group-defense damage reductions per tick. mean_shelter_depth — mean shelter depth across occupied cells. mean_mutation_rate — mean evolved mutation_sd mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE. mean_clutch_size — mean clutch size clutch_size_evolution = TRUE.

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)
text: Social learning warning: run_alife() now warns social_learning = TRUE n_agents_init < 100, since neighbour density population size rarely sufficient trigger copying events.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: First public release.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: Agent-based evolutionary simulation running entirely Julia via JuliaConnectoR, eliminating per-tick R/Julia boundary overhead. Toroidal grid logistic grass regrowth seasonal amplitude modulation. Diploid haploid genome meiosis, Mendelian dominance models, configurable crossover mutation rates. Six brain types: Bayesian neural network (BNN, default), multilayer perceptron (ANN), continuous-time RNN (CTRNN), gene regulatory network (GRN), random null model, stubs transformer synthesis brains. run_alife() — single simulation run; returns named list compatible get_run_data() plot_run(). batch_alife() — run multiple replicates sequence. default_specs() — fully documented parameter list sensible defaults.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: module -op flag FALSE; overhead zero. Disease / SIR (disease = TRUE): stochastic transmission, energy costs, recovery, immunity. Kin selection (kin_selection = TRUE): pedigree-based relatedness; donors transfer energy -related Moore-neighbourhood agent relatedness threshold. Cooperation (cooperation = TRUE): reciprocal altruism mediated heritable cooperation-level trait. Scavenging (scavenging = TRUE): carrion deposited agent death; scavengers gain energy decaying carcasses. Niche construction (niche_construction = TRUE): agents build shelters slow grass regrowth reduce predator damage; shelters decay stochastically. Epigenetics (epigenetics = TRUE): heritable methylation marks canalize BNN weight uncertainty (sigma); transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) transmits marks offspring configurable probability. Within-lifetime RL (rl_mode = "actor_critic"): REINFORCE baseline updates output layer agent’s neural network tick, driven energy-gain reward. Social learning (social_learning = TRUE): prestige-biased copying — agents blend fraction highest-energy neighbour’s output-layer weights policy. Brain size evolution (brain_size_evolution = TRUE): heritable brain_size trait (Float32, reference 1.0) modelling parental provisioning hypothesis (van Schaik et al. 2023; Griesser et al. 2023; Song et al. 2025). Larger brains incur per-tick idle-cost surcharge (expensive brain hypothesis) proportional cognitive foraging bonus. bootstrapping problem — large-brained offspring pay metabolic cost birth foraging advantage emerges — means brain size evolves parental_care = TRUE buffers infancy energy deficit. third effect — sensing quality — scales grass perception inputs brain_size ^ brain_size_sensing_exponent, giving larger-brained agents directional navigation advantage. Logged mean_brain_size env$progress. New parameters: brain_size_evolution, brain_size_init_mean, brain_size_mutation_sd, brain_size_min, brain_size_max, brain_size_cost_scale, brain_size_sensing_exponent.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: search_map_elites() — MAP-Elites quality-diversity search simulation parameters (Mouret & Clune 2015). Returns archive parameter sets covering behavioural descriptor space. search_cmaes() — CMA-ES optimisation via GA package. search_gradient() — finite-difference gradient ascent log parameter scale; backend-agnostic (Zygote.jl dependency).

Full test suite covers genome, brains (ANN, BNN, CTRNN, GRN), disease, kin, cooperation, scavenging, niche, analysis helpers, visualization.
